text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled beneath ancient oaks, lived a timid rabbit named Rufus. He had an especially fluffy tail with a bright red spot on it—a badge of his pride. Rufus's days were filled with the ordinary tasks of rabbit life: munching on herbs for a daily dose of vitamins, visiting Mr. Tortoise's sundial every morning to admire its ever-changing face.

One sunny afternoon, Rufus was admiring himself in a small mirror he found among the rocks. He was so pleased with his red spot that he thought of himself as a bright light in a darker world.

Just then, a shadow fell across their valley. It was the Great Owl, known far and wide for her wisdom. With a deep, throat-calling horn, she spoke:

"Rabbit of great redness, you flutter with vanity, but remember, even the moon lacks your brightness under the night sky."
